title: 10 best herbal tinctures for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for numbness in hands are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hands. These natural remedies help to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and relax nervous system tension, providing relief from numbness caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome, neuropathy, and poor nerve function. Examples include St. John's Wort, Ginkgo biloba, and Ashwagandha tinctures, which have been shown to improve hand dexterity, reduce pain and discomfort, and enhance overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as adenosine and caffeic acid, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve microcirculation, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the affected areas. This increased blood flow can help to alleviate numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hands, providing relief from symptoms associated with conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome and Raynaud's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Maidenhair tree tincture's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to numbness in hands by protecting nerve cells from damage.

Vasodilatory property: The tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels helps to improve circulation, increasing oxygen and nutrient supply to the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by reduced blood flow.

Neuroprotective property: Maidenhair tree contains compounds that have been shown to protect against neurodegeneration and promote nerve regeneration, making it potentially beneficial for treating numbness in hands caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or carpal tunnel syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them effective for relieving numbness in the hands.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the maidenhair tree are rich in bioactive compounds that stimulate nerve activity, helping to restore sensation and alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Measure out 30 grams of the powdered ginkgo biloba and combine it with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the bottle daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the finished ginkgo biloba tincture in an amber glass bottle at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its remarkable ability to soothe and calm the nervous system. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often underlying causes of numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds help to relax muscle tension, improving blood flow and oxygenation to the extremities. This natural combination of actions helps to alleviate numbness and restore a sense of comfort and normal sensation in the hands.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic activity: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to numbness in hands by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands, which can cause numbness and tingling sensations by reducing oxidative stress and promoting blood flow.

Neuroprotective properties: Ashwagandha contains neuroprotective compounds that may help protect against damage to nerves and promote healthy nerve function, potentially alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by supporting nerve health and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in withanolides, a bioactive compound that helps to improve circulation and reduce numbness in hands.
Barks: Contains alkaloids like cusiagenin and withaferin A, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh withania somnifera roots in the early morning, after ensuring they have not been sprayed with pesticides or herbicides.
Clean and chop 100 grams of harvested root material into small pieces to facilitate efficient extraction.
Combine chopped root material with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the root material is completely submerged.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days at room temperature, shaking the jar daily to promote efficient extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves and joints. The curcuminoids present in turmeric also improve circulation and reduce oxidative stress, allowing for a more efficient transmission of nerve signals. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids, reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve endings, which can contribute to numbness in hands caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: The active compounds in turmeric, particularly curcumin, have natural pain-relieving and analgesic properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve numbness in hands.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, alleviating numbness in hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots with a high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Clean the turmeric roots thoroughly and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the chopped turmeric roots with 70% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar covering the roots completely.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least six weeks shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pressure on nerves. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, also improve blood circulation to the extremities, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach nerve endings more efficiently. As a result, herbal ginger tinctures can effectively alleviate numbness in hands caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome, peripheral neuropathy, or Raynaud's disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can contribute to numbness in the hands by reducing pressure on nerves.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's ability to dilate blood vessels increases blood flow to affected areas, which can help restore oxygenation and nutrient delivery to nerves and tissues, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Ginger's analgesic compounds, such as gingerol and paradol, help reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands, making it easier for individuals to perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the thick, underground stems that store nutrients and have a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an effective source of medicinally active molecules for treating numbness.
Roots: Ginger roots contain volatile oils and phenolic compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate numbness in hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root from your garden or purchase from a reputable source.
Clean and chop the ginger root into small pieces weighing approximately 500 grams.
Combine chopped ginger root with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka and store in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, before straining and discarding solids.
Filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, using a clean dropper to fill each bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to promote nerve health and improve blood circulation. The herb, also known as Bacopa monnieri, is traditionally used to enhance cognitive function and memory, but it also has a profound impact on the nervous system. By increasing the production of neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress, Brahmi tinctures can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, allowing for improved sensation and reduced discomfort. Regular use of Brahmi tincture may also lead to enhanced nerve function and reduced risk of nerve damage.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi tincture's ability to enhance cognitive function, including memory and concentration, can help alleviate numbness in hands by improving overall nervous system function and reducing symptoms of neuropathy.

Neuroprotective Properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds have been shown to prevent damage to nerve cells and promote their growth, which can help reduce numbness in hands caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or nerve damage.

Anti-Inflammatory and Antioxidant Properties: Brahmi tincture's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to numbness in hands by reducing damage to nerve cells and improving overall nervous system function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Brahmi plant have been traditionally used to treat numbness and tingling sensations in the hands due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Brahmi contain saponins, which are believed to help alleviate numbness and pain in the hands by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems for optimal potency and quality ensuring sufficient material.
Cleanse and sanitize the harvested plant material by rinsing with filtered water and air-drying for two days.
Chop the dried bacopa monnieri into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped herb with 70% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent steeping for six weeks.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to increase blood flow and circulation. The bioactive compounds in St John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve microcirculation, which can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate any underlying inflammation or irritation contributing to numbness, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being in the affected area.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to numbness in hands.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hypericin and hyperforin, can dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help increase oxygen and nutrient delivery to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.

Neuroprotective properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, including the ability to reduce inflammation in the nervous system and protect against neuronal damage, which can help alleviate numbness and other symptoms associated with neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving numbness and pain in the hands.
Buds: The buds contain high concentrations of hyperforin, a compound known to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak blooming for maximum potency.
Cleanse and chop the harvested flowers into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine one part chopped flowers with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling to the top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains a unique blend of herbal extracts that stimulate blood flow and circulation to the extremities. The potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds found in bell peppers work to soothe and calm irritated nerve endings, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the natural vasodilating properties help to widen blood vessels, allowing for increased oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the hands, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Bell peppers contain high levels of antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsaicin present in bell peppers has natural anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with numbness, allowing for improved blood flow and nerve function.

Neuroprotective compounds: Bell peppers contain neuroprotective compounds like vitamin C and carotenoids that help protect the nerves from damage and promote healthy nerve function, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and numbness in the hands by reducing swelling and inflammation.
Fruits: The fruits of the bell pepper plant contain antioxidants and vitamins A and C, which can help improve circulation and reduce numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-75 grams of fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by washing it gently with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Air dry the cleaned plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 1-2 days.
Combine 10 parts of dried capsicum annuum leaves and stems with 90 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids, and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, it reduces inflammation and swelling in the body, which can help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or nerve damage. The tincture also improves circulation, allowing oxygenated blood to reach the nerves more efficiently, which helps to reduce numbness and promote healthy nerve function.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main active compound, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids like licoricidin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect nerve cells from damage and promote healthy blood flow to the extremities, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.
Saponins: Saponins present in licorice root have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving numbness and pain in hands.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like glycerrhetic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots according to traditional guidelines and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned root material into small pieces and mix it with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of one part herb to two parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four to six weeks, shaking the container daily during this period.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles.
Label and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to one year, dosing as needed for numbness relief at 20-30 drops per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains a natural sedative property that soothes and calms the nervous system. By relaxing the nerves, valerian tincture can help reduce feelings of numbness, tingling, and pins and needles in the hands. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to reducing swelling and discomfort associated with conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome, allowing individuals to regain sensation and control in their hands.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for peripheral neuropathy.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which may contribute to numbness in hands.
Pain-relieving properties: Valerian has been shown to have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help relieve pain and numbness in hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of valepotriates, a group of compounds that help to increase blood flow and relieve numbness and tingling sensations.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of essential oils, including valerenic acid, which helps to improve circulation and reduce numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and rinse them thoroughly with filtered water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and mix with 40% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least four hours in a cool dark place to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them clearly with dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains a unique blend of bioactive compounds that work together to stimulate blood flow and alleviate circulatory issues. The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb help to improve oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the affected areas, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, asiatic pennywort has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with numbness. As a result, users of the tincture may experience significant relief from numbness in their hands.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by protecting nerve cells from damage.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Asiatic Pennywort tinctures help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected areas, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations by improving blood flow and reducing pressure on nerves.

Neuroprotective Properties: Asiatic Pennywort's neuroprotective properties may help protect and regenerate damaged nerve cells, promoting healthy neural function and potentially alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by improving communication between neurons.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots have been traditionally used to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands due to their ability to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation.

Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the Asiatic pennywort plant, known as rhyzomes, contain a wealth of medicinal properties that help to relieve numbness and pain in the hands by reducing nerve inflammation and improving nerve conduction.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality centella asiatica leaves from a pesticide-free environment weighing approximately 20% of the final desired yield.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and combine them with 70% vodka or other high-proof grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six to eight weeks shaking the jar twice daily to ensure thorough maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer the liquid tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for at least two weeks before use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat numbness in hands with herbal tinctures is to combine Ginkgo biloba, Bupleurum, and Corydalis tinctures. Ginkgo biloba improves circulation and enhances nerve function, while Bupleurum reduces inflammation and calms the nervous system. Corydalis eases pain and numbness by stimulating nerve endings. Mix equal parts of each tincture in a ratio of 20-30 drops taken three times a day with water or food. This blend can help alleviate numbness in hands by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and stimulating nerve function.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome, nerve damage, and Raynaud's disease. Herbal tinctures like St. John's Wort, Ginkgo biloba, and Ginger root have been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of numbness, tingling, and cold intolerance in the hands and fingers. These herbal remedies may help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote nerve health, providing relief from discomfort and numbness.
